title: chargeback — HTTP routes
description: Tenant-facing admin+finance surfaces only — customers NEVER interact with chargebacks over the tenant HTTP surface. Chargebacks are bank-initiated + customer-driven at the bank; surfacing chargeback state to the customer may violate some card network rules. Admin+finance surfaces cover list, detail, evidence view + submission, RDR accept, manual mark-lost, pending-evidence queue, chargeback-rate report. Platform-plane cross-tenant read exists for support intervention + regulator-facing reporting + merchant-account risk monitoring (rate-threshold alerts). Every tenant route carries `entitlement.enforce:chargeback_management`; evidence submission additionally carries `entitlement.enforce:chargeback_evidence_submission`. RDR accept additionally carries `entitlement.enforce:chargeback_rdr`.

groups.name: tenant-chargebacks-admin
groups.description: Admin/finance chargeback management. Read-heavy — chargebacks are bank-initiated, so no POST /chargebacks route exists (creation is via CreateChargebackFromDisputeJob OR ChargebackWebhookReceived). Actions: evidence submission (POST /{chargeback}/evidence), RDR accept (POST /{chargeback}/accept), manual mark-lost (POST /{chargeback}/mark-lost).

routes.description: filter[status], filter[network], filter[reason_category], filter[reason_code], filter[provider], filter[rdr_eligible] (bool), filter[ce3_eligible] (bool), filter[filed_at.gte/lte], filter[amount_cents.gte/lte], filter[payment_id], filter[invoice_id], filter[evidence_due_by.lte] (surfaces approaching-deadline chargebacks). include=payment_dispute,transaction,fee_transaction,refund,latest_evidence,payment,invoice. Full-tenant chargeback ledger.

Single chargeback detail. Response includes: base fields + parent payment + parent invoice + linked payment_dispute + linked transaction (loss offset) + linked fee_transaction (network fee) + linked refund (when RDR path) + latest chargeback_evidence + reconciliation history + downstream clawback status (referral + coupon).

Every ChargebackEvidence submission for a specific chargeback (all submission attempts, ordered by submission_attempt_number ASC). Evidence bundles may contain customer PII — segregated permission (chargebacks.view-evidence) required beyond chargebacks.view.

Body: ChargebackEvidenceCreateData { invoice_evidence, receipt_evidence, service_documentation, refund_policy, communication_log, shipping_docs?, customer_signature_evidence, uncategorized_text? }. Auto-increments submission_attempt_number. Refuses when parent chargeback.status NOT IN ('pending_evidence', 'evidence_submitted') OR evidence_due_by < NOW(). Refuses when evidence body contains PAN patterns (PCI-DSS Req 3.4). Dispatches SubmitChargebackEvidenceJob async — the observer creates the row, the job sends to the bank.

RDR path — auto-refund below threshold. Body: { notes? }. Only allowed when parent chargeback.rdr_eligible=true AND status='pending_evidence'. Delegates to RdrHandler::accept which creates a Refund via refund::RefundOrchestrator. On RefundSucceeded, chargeback transitions to accepted_refund (terminal). Fires ChargebackAcceptedRefund. Cheapest defense — no network fee, no rate impact.

Admin escape hatch — force-mark a chargeback as lost when ops determined defense is not viable. Body: { decision_reason (256 char, required), notes? }. Only allowed when status IN ('pending_evidence', 'evidence_submitted', 'under_bank_review'). Dispatches FinalizeChargebackJob + fires ChargebackLost. Chargebacks in provider-side won/lost states cannot be manually overridden — those are provider-authoritative.

Chargebacks in status='pending_evidence' sorted by evidence_due_by ASC (soonest deadlines first). filter[days_remaining_lte]. filter[days_remaining_gte]. Includes days_remaining + current_evidence_completeness_percent + assigned_defenders (from metadata.evidence_notification) for triage.

Per-tenant chargeback rate rollup. Query params: period_start, period_end (default: last 60 days — matches rate-monitoring rolling window). Response: { rolling_rate_by_network (map<network,percent>), threshold_by_network, threshold_status (below / approaching / exceeded), total_chargebacks, total_lost, total_won, total_fees_cents, reason_category_distribution, reason_code_top_10, provider_distribution, rdr_acceptance_count, ce3_evidence_submission_count, median_days_to_decision, win_rate_percent }. Confidential financial fields redacted when caller lacks chargebacks.view-financials.

groups.name: platform-chargebacks-support
groups.description: Cross-tenant chargeback read for support intervention + regulator-facing reporting + merchant-account risk monitoring. Customer PII hashed unless the caller holds platform.chargeback.pii.view.

routes.description: filter[tenant_id], filter[status], filter[network], filter[reason_category], filter[filed_at.gte/lte]. Cross-tenant scan.

Cross-tenant merchant-account risk monitoring. Surfaces every tenant with current rate exceeding the network's VDMP/MDMP threshold (0.9% Visa / 1.5% Mastercard / 1.0% Amex / 1.0% Discover). filter[network]. filter[days_over_threshold_gte]. Actionable — Academorix ops must intervene when tenants sustain over-threshold rates (fraud review, policy update, RDR enrollment recommendation, or in extreme cases account termination).

groups.name: provider-webhooks
groups.description: Provider webhook receipt. Owned by the payment module's PaymentWebhookHandler + PaymentWebhookVerifier — this module contributes chargeback-typed event handling via listener (HandleChargebackWebhook). No routes declared here — the payment module owns the route + this module hooks into ChargebackWebhookReceived events fired by the payment module's webhook handler.
